Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to craft a SQL query for DuckDB that returns all events from event_log table except those belonging to cases that have the exact sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession and where the elapsed time between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we need to filter out those case_ids that meet both conditions Then return all events from other cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user did not specify which columns but they said return all events from event_log So presumably select all

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can use window functions to identify sequences Approach Use LAG or LEAD functions to find for each event ordered by timestamp the next activity and the next two activities after it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then identify those rows where activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition and next1 activity equals Create Order and next2 activity equals Confirm Order That is direct succession with no events in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then capture the case_id and also record the timestamp of the Create Order and Confirm Order events Then compute difference next2 timestamp minus next1 timestamp greater than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then collect distinct case_ids that meet both conditions Then exclude those case_ids from the final output using a where case_id not in subquery

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternative Use a subquery to compute flagged cases maybe using with

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If there are multiple occurrences of the sequence in same case the flagged_cases subquery will capture case_id distinct It does not matter because we exclude that case entirely

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In DuckDB subtracting timestamps yields interval and comparing with interval 5 days works

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Cases where there are multiple Approve Purchase Requisition events need to consider any of them that start the sequence

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Cases where Create Order and Confirm Order happen more than once need to find any pair where they are in direct succession and the time elapsed is more than 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That is also covered because we check for any row that is Approve Purchase Requisition and next1 is Create Order and next2 is Confirm Order

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If there is also a Confirm Order after Create Order but not in immediate succession like something else between the query will not capture that because we require direct

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can also incorporate a direct join not exists Both are fine
